LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS RONNIE BELL AND MONICA BELL'S MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES FROM COMPLAINT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANT ACTED "WITH A WILLFUL AND CONSCIOUS DISREGARD OF THE RIGHTS OR SAFETY OF OTHERS." CAL. CIVIL CODE SEC. 3294(C)(1). Motion For Order To Compel Nueropsycholigical Exam Without Recording Of Testing
Off Calendar Sep-01-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. NIKOLAUS W. REED OF NWR, TEL. NO. 1-415-940-7766 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JOHN D. HOURIHAN OF THE STRATMAN, PATTERSON & HUNTER, TEL. NO. 1-415-940-7766 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED REGARDING CHANGES TO THE PROPOSED ORDER. DEFENDANTS RONNIE BELL AND MONICA BELL'S Motion For Order To Compel Nueropsycholigical Exam Without Recording Of Testing - Defendants' motion is granted in part and denied in part (See Order for details) Order signed in open court. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Permitting Discovery Of Defendant Ronnie Bells Financial Condition
Off Calendar Apr-16-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JOHN D. HOURIHAN of Stratman, Patterson & Hunter, Tel No. (510) 457-3455 appearing for Defendant Ronnie Bell;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF DANEAL HARRIS' Motion For Order Permitting Discovery Of Defendant Ronnie Bells Financial Condition is denied. Plaintiff has not shown that he has a substantial probability of establishing that Mr. Bell acted maliciously, oppressively and/or fraudulently. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/HK)

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Dec-17-2014 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From Complaint (With First Appearance Fee)
Off Calendar Oct-23-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS RONNIE BELL and MONICA BELL's Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From Complaint is denied. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts to show that defendant acted "with a willful and conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others." Cal. Civil Code sec. 3294(c)(1). Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
